content.problemStatement:
As we all know, Chefland consists of NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) connected by N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 roads in such a way that each city is reachable from all other cities. In other words, the cities and roads form a tree. For each valid iii, the iii-th city's height above sea level is AiA_iAi‚Äã.

A path between two cities is said to be beautiful if the heights of the cities in the path first increase and then decrease. In other words, when we denote the sequence of cities the path passes through by x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xLx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Lx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xL‚Äã, it is beautiful if Ax1<Ax2<‚Ä¶<Axp>‚Ä¶>AxL‚àí1>AxLA_{x_1} \lt A_{x_2} \lt \ldots \lt A_{x_p} \gt \ldots \gt A_{x_{L-1}} \gt A_{x_L}Ax1‚Äã‚Äã<Ax2‚Äã‚Äã<‚Ä¶<Axp‚Äã‚Äã>‚Ä¶>AxL‚àí1‚Äã‚Äã>AxL‚Äã‚Äã holds for some integer ppp (1‚â§p‚â§L1 \le p \le L1‚â§p‚â§L).

Chefina is planning a trip along a path in the tree. You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given two cities xxx and yyy and you should tell Chefina if the simple path between these cities is beautiful.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
Each of the following N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that cities uuu and vvv are connected by a road.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing a string with length QQQ. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string should be '1' if the path in the iii-th query is beautiful or '0' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The sequence of heights of cities on the path from city 111 to city 555 is [4,5][4, 5][4,5], and 4<54 \lt 54<5 holds, so it is a beautiful path.
Example case 2: The sequence of heights of cities on the path from city 333 to city 444 is [2,6,1][2, 6, 1][2,6,1], and 2<6>12 \lt 6 \gt 12<6>1, so it is a beautiful path.
